Common.tagline: ID photos, done right in your browser

Home.heroTitle: ID photos, done right in your browser
Home.heroSubtitle: Upload a portrait, then cut out, swap the background, crop to spec, lay out for printing, compress, and export — all without ever uploading your photo.
Home.ctaHint: Free · No sign-up · 100% in your browser

privacy.title: Privacy first
privacy.desc: Every step runs on your device. Your photos never leave the browser.

smart.title: Smart crop
smart.desc: Faces and shoulders are auto-aligned to each country's spec.

layout.title: One-click layout
layout.desc: Auto-tile onto 4x6 or 5x7 paper with crop marks ready for the print shop.

compress.title: Precise compression
compress.desc: Binary-search compression to hit the exact KB range visa portals require.

uploadDropzone.title: Drop a photo here, or click to choose
uploadDropzone.subtitle: JPG / PNG / HEIC · up to 50 MB

Footer.tagline: ID photo studio inside your browser

Studio.title: Studio
Studio.subtitle: Upload a portrait to cut out, recolor the background, crop, and lay out.

empty.title: No photo yet
empty.subtitle: Upload a portrait to get started

tabs.flowHint: Step through in order — or jump to any tab any time.

Crop.title: Photo spec
Crop.subtitle: Pick a spec to lock the aspect ratio and auto-center the head.
Crop.customDpiPxHint: DPI is fixed when entering pixels directly.

detect.failed: Face detection failed — drag the frame manually.
detect.noFace: No face detected — falling back to a centred crop.
detect.autoCentered: Auto-centred. Drag the crop frame to fine-tune.

warnings.title: Composition hints
warnings.head-too-small: Head is small: currently {ratio}% — spec asks for ~{lo}%–{hi}%
warnings.head-too-large: Head is large: currently {ratio}% — spec asks for ~{lo}%–{hi}%
warnings.eye-too-high: Eyes too high: currently {ratio}% from top — spec asks for ~{lo}%–{hi}%
warnings.eye-too-low: Eyes too low: currently {ratio}% from top — spec asks for ~{lo}%–{hi}%
warnings.face-not-found: No face detected — can't check composition.

Background.title: Background color

cutout.title: Cut out the subject first
cutout.hint: Background colour changes need the subject isolated. Run cut-out now, or pick a colour and we'll apply it once you do.

Background.compareHint: Drag the divider to compare backgrounds

Export.title: Export
Export.subtitle: Pick a format, dial in the quality, and download.
Export.qualityMaxHint: JPG / WebP at 100% is still lossy — pick PNG for true lossless.
Export.dimensionsModeSpec: Sized for the selected ID-photo spec (print-ready).
Export.dimensionsModeSpecWithRatio: Sized per spec · pixels shrunk ~{ratio}× (that's why the file is small).
Export.dimensionsModeNative: Keeps the crop's native resolution (larger file, reprintable at any size).
Export.keepNativeHint: Export at the crop frame's original pixel size. The file gets much larger, but the same crop can be reprinted at any size.
Export.qualityLockedByCompress: Quality is set automatically while compressing to a target size.
Export.compressUnsupported: Compression only applies to JPG / WebP.
Export.kbHint: We binary-search quality and downscale if necessary to land in ±5% of the target.
Export.compressMiss: Closest result was {kb} KB — try a larger target or downscale further.
Export.alphaNeedsCutout: PNG with transparency needs the subject cut out first.
Export.alphaCutoutHint: Want a transparent background? Cut out the subject first.
Export.alphaGoToBackground: Open the Background tab to cut out →
Export.cropAppliedHint: Cropped to your selected spec — adjust on the Size tab if needed.
Export.downloadFailed: Couldn't produce the file — try a different format.
Export.fullResHint: Preserves original resolution — the export may take a couple of seconds.

hint.alpha: Alpha-capable formats keep the transparent background intact.
hint.flat: Flat formats bake the current background colour into the export.

Export.wechatHint: Inside WeChat? Long-press the preview to save it to your album.

Paper.title: Paper
Paper.subtitle: Pick the print paper your photos will tile onto.

list.heading: Print paper sizes
list.subtitle: Standard print-shop papers Pixfit supports for tiled layouts. Pick one in Studio to auto-tile your photos.
list.intro: From 3R to A4, the seven built-in papers cover every common Chinese print-shop format plus office printers. All sizes are calibrated to 300 DPI so what you see is what gets printed.
list.metaTitle: Print paper sizes for ID photo layouts · Pixfit
list.metaDescription: Browse the seven built-in print papers (3R / 4R / 5R / 6R / 8R / A4 / A5) Pixfit uses to tile and export ID photo layouts.

templates.title: Layout
templates.subtitle: Pick a builtin arrangement compatible with the chosen paper.
templates.empty: No builtin layouts fit this paper yet — drop into mixed mode below.

mix.title: Mixed photos
mix.subtitle: Override the template with your own count per spec.

settings.title: Layout settings
settings.subtitle: Margins, gaps, separators and crop marks.

preview.overflow: Some photos didn't fit: {list}. Try a larger paper or thinner gaps.

actions.title: Download
actions.subtitle: PNG keeps it raster; PDF stays vector for print shops.
actions.downloadFailed: Couldn't produce the layout — try a smaller paper or fewer photos.

Sizes.heading: ID photo sizes
Sizes.subtitle: Every built-in photo spec Pixfit ships with — Chinese IDs, travel permits, popular visas, and exam-registration formats.
Sizes.intro: Twenty-eight specs covering Chinese 1- and 2-inch IDs, Hong Kong / Macao / Taiwan travel permits, fourteen common visa standards (US / Schengen / UK / CA / AU / NZ / JP / KR / SG / MY / VN / TH / RU), and three national exam registration formats. Pick one to lock the aspect ratio in Studio and auto-centre the head.
Sizes.categoryEmpty: No specs in this category yet
Sizes.metaTitle: ID photo sizes for visa, ID, and exam registration · Pixfit
Sizes.metaDescription: Browse 28 built-in ID photo specifications — US / Schengen / UK / CA / AU / NZ / JP / KR visas, Chinese 1- and 2-inch IDs, travel permits, and exam formats. Free, in-browser, no upload.

Templates.heading: Layout templates
Templates.subtitle: Auto-grid arrangements that tile your photos onto a single print paper, ready for the print shop.
Templates.intro: Twelve built-in templates covering the most common print-shop scenarios: 8× / 16× 1-inch, 8× 2-inch, mixed 1+2-inch sets, ID cards, passports, and wallet photos. Each template picks the right paper, computes the grid, and exports as PNG or vector-clean PDF.
Templates.metaTitle: ID photo print layout templates · Pixfit
Templates.metaDescription: Browse twelve built-in print layouts — 8 × 1-inch, 16 × 1-inch, mixed 1+2-inch, ID card, passport, and wallet — across 5R / 6R / A4 papers. Export PNG or vector PDF.

privacy.heading: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: Pixfit runs entirely in your browser. We do not collect, store, or transmit your photos.
privacy.metaDescription: How Pixfit handles your data: every image stays on your device, no cookies for tracking, and the only persistent storage is your own customised specs in localStorage.

summary.title: In short
summary.body: Pixfit is a browser-only ID photo studio. Your photos never leave your device. We do not run accounts, do not require sign-up, and do not track you with cookies.

data.title: What we process
data.body: When you upload a photo, the file is decoded in your browser, processed by an on-device AI model (MODNet running on WebGPU / WebAssembly), and rendered onto a local canvas. None of those bytes are sent over the network.

localStorage.title: Local storage
localStorage.body: We use your browser's localStorage only to remember the photo / paper / layout specs you create or import (key `pixfit:specs:v1`) plus a small list of recently-used background colours. You can clear them at any time from your browser's site-settings panel.

thirdParty.title: Third-party resources
thirdParty.body: The AI model weights, fonts, and the MediaPipe runtime are fetched from public mirrors (ModelScope, jsDelivr, the Hugging Face CDN). These services see only the model file requests — never your photos. Once cached your subsequent visits are fully offline-capable for the core flow.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.body: Once we go live we plan to use Cloudflare Web Analytics, which is cookie-less and does not collect IP addresses or device fingerprints. No script is loaded until that decision ships. No third-party analytics tracker (Google / Mixpanel / Sentry) is bundled today.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: Because we do not collect personal data, there is nothing for us to delete or export on your behalf. You retain full control by clearing local site data inside your browser.

children.title: Children
children.body: Pixfit is suitable for general audiences but is not directed at children under 13. If you are using Pixfit on behalf of a minor, please review these terms with their guardian.

changes.title: Updates to this policy
changes.body: If our data-handling practices ever change we will update this page and bump the last-updated date. Material changes will also be summarised on the home page or via the in-app notification banner.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Open an issue at github.com/pixfit/pixfit or email the maintainer listed in the repository README.

terms.heading: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Pixfit is offered free of charge, as-is, and with no warranties. By using it you agree to these terms.
terms.metaDescription: Pixfit is free and open source. You agree to use it only with photos you have the right to process, and accept the software is provided as-is.

scope.title: Scope
scope.body: These terms cover any use of pix-fit.com and the open-source Pixfit code base. By loading the site or running the code you agree to be bound by them.

service.title: What we provide
service.body: A browser-based ID photo studio. Every feature runs locally; the host service exists only to serve static files. We do not promise any specific level of availability or accuracy.

responsibility.title: Your responsibility
responsibility.body: You must hold the rights to every photo you process. Do not upload other people's photos without their consent and do not use Pixfit to violate any government photo standard, identity-verification policy, or local law.

compliance.title: Spec accuracy
compliance.body: Built-in photo specifications are cross-referenced from official sources at the time of writing. Embassies and exam boards change their rules regularly — please double-check the latest official requirement before submitting your application.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.body: The Pixfit source code is released under the MIT license. Built-in icons, fonts, and the AI model are licensed by their respective authors (see the README). Your photos remain yours.

warranty.title: No warranty
warranty.body: The service is provided as-is and as-available, without warranties of any kind, express or implied. We disclaim warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of Pixfit, including but not limited to rejected applications or printing costs.

changes.title: Changes to the service
changes.body: We may add, change, or remove features at any time. Material changes will be announced through the changelog in the open-source repository.

law.title: Governing law
law.body: Disputes are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction where the maintainer resides, and where applicable, by the relevant local consumer-protection law.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Open an issue at github.com/pixfit/pixfit for any term-related question.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for has been moved or never existed. Head back to the studio or the home page to keep going.

runtime.title: Something went wrong
runtime.body: Pixfit hit an unexpected error rendering this page. The issue has been logged in your browser console. Try retrying or reload the page; if it persists, please open an issue.

SpecManager.title: Manage specs
SpecManager.subtitle: Manage your photo specs, paper specs, and layout templates. Built-in entries are read-only — duplicate one to start customising.

list.builtinHint: Built-in specs are read-only — duplicate one to create an editable copy

fields.idHint: Lowercase letters, numbers and dashes — cannot be changed after creation

toast.duplicated: Created copy “{name}” based on the built-in
toast.validationError: The form has invalid fields — please review before saving

delete.title: Delete “{name}”?
delete.body: This will remove the custom entry. Built-in entries are not affected.
delete.withDependentsBody: {count} layout template(s) reference this spec. After deletion they will fall back to the matching built-in (if any) or become invalid.

import.title: Import specs
import.hint: Choose a JSON file exported from Pixfit
import.preview: Will import {photo} photo / {paper} paper / {layout} layout entries (replaces your existing customisations)

errors.unsupported-version: Unsupported version. Pixfit only imports version 1.

layout.comingSoon: The layout-template editor arrives with M6. For now you can import / export / delete existing templates, but creation is disabled.

empty.title: Select an entry on the left to view its details
empty.subtitle: Or click “New” to create your own spec

errors.title: Couldn't extract the subject
errors.network: Network error while downloading the model. Check your connection and retry.
errors.integrity: The model file failed its integrity check. The connection may have been tampered with — please reload and try again.
errors.init: The inference engine failed to start. Your browser may not support a required feature.
errors.inference: Image processing failed. Try a different photo.
errors.unknown: An unknown error occurred. Please reload the page.
